behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with a descriptive error message.
behaviors.justification: Boundary proof: stays at Unit because the failure is local parser validation/message formatting logic within one object boundary. No concrete module handoff is required by this behavior text. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because the behavior text is only about parser rejection/output message, not parser->other-module interaction; System rejected because no OS/network/subprocess/external dependency is mentioned; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Call parse(cron_string) directly with invalid inputs (e.g., '0 0 1 13 *', '0 0 32 * *', malformed tokenization). Assert it returns/raises structured errors that include the offending field and reason.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including 'last day of month' and 'nearest weekday.'
behaviors.justification: Boundary proof Unit -> Integration: real failure can occur at parser->calculator handoff (e.g., parser encoding for 'last day'/'nearest weekday' interpreted incorrectly by calculator), which a pure single-function test can miss. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; no AI/ML output; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is deterministic timestamp computation from cron expressions, not real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real parser + schedule calculator in-process. Feed cron strings using L/W semantics and a fixed start time; compute next N runs and assert exact expected timestamps across month boundaries and weekday adjustments.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector identifies schedules firing within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Boundary proof Unit -> Integration: failure appears when schedule-generation outputs are handed to overlap detection (e.g., timestamp normalization/granularity mismatch across modules), not only inside overlap math. Integration brake: no OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-step agentic flow. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior specifies deterministic schedule comparison and reporting, not real external infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Use real schedule generation for two cron inputs plus detector module with min_gap=5m. Assert detector flags conflicts only when deltas are below threshold and returns correct conflict window boundaries.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job correctly.
behaviors.justification: Boundary proof Unit -> Integration: failure is at timezone-rule application handoff between schedule calculation and timezone-aware datetime handling (policy + time library interaction), not just one local arithmetic function. Integration brake: behavior can be validated in-process with a real timezone library; it does not require real network/subprocess/OS event behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because the behavior text requires DST-correct timestamps, which can be deterministically tested with timezone data in-process; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + calculator + timezone conversion together for a DST-observing zone. For spring-forward date, assert configured policy for nonexistent 2:30 AM time; for fall-back date, assert correct handling of ambiguous 2:30 AM occurrence(s) per engine policy.

behaviors.description: If computed next run time has already passed (downtime), engine applies missed-run policy to skip or backfill.
behaviors.justification: Boundary proof Unit -> Integration: failure occurs at handoff between next-run computation and policy executor (computed time comparison + policy decision path), which is cross-module state logic. Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; no AI/ML quality dependence; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejection gate: System rejected because the behavior is policy routing based on computed timestamps, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Compose scheduler calculator with missed-run policy module in-process. Simulate 'now' after next_run for both policies (SKIP, BACKFILL) and assert selected action plus resulting scheduled times/job instances.
